skill.name: ui-design-system
skill.description: UI design system toolkit for creating design tokens, component documentation, responsive scales, and developer handoff references.
skill.summary: Create design token systems for colors, typography, spacing, sizing, borders, shadows, animation, and breakpoints.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The high static score is driven by false positives around design-system terminology, CSS color values, and markdown code formatting. No evidence found of malware intent, network access, credential access, cryptography misuse, filesystem writes, shell execution, or prompt injection. The remaining risk is low because the community package includes a local Python helper script.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Windows SAM Database Match
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM database alerts are false positives. In the script, the match comes from the text DESIGN SYSTEM TOKENS. In marketplace_content.json, the phrase appears only inside prior audit text.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss this static finding. It does not indicate credential database access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is visible in benign output and audit prose. No code opens Windows paths, reads account databases, or accesses host credentials.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword alerts are false positives. The script match is inside the UI error color hex value #DC2626, and the marketplace_content.json matches are quoted audit text.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss this static finding. No command-and-control behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only script occurrence is a CSS color literal. No socket, HTTP client, webhook, beacon, or remote endpoint code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from words such as token, generate, and system. These files discuss design tokens and output formatting, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss this static finding. The terminology is standard design-system vocabulary.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Python file imports json, typing, colorsys, and sys only. No md5, sha1, cipher, key derivation, random token generation, or credential handling code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Term
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The word system refers to a design system and an 8-point grid system, not host or network enumeration.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss this static finding. No reconnaissance logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain no host discovery, process listing, username lookup, filesystem inventory, or network scanning behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Usage
low_findings.description: The external command alert is a false positive. SKILL.md uses markdown backticks to format a documented Python usage command, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.recommendation: Dismiss this static finding. Treat it as documentation formatting only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The usage line is markdown text. The Python file does not call subprocess, os.system, shell=True, or equivalent external command APIs.

content.value_statement: Design systems need consistent decisions across color, type, spacing, and responsive behavior. This skill helps create token structures and handoff references from a small set of brand inputs.
actual_capabilities: Defines color token groups for primary, secondary, neutral, semantic, and surface palettes.
Defines typography tokens for font families, sizes, weights, line heights, letter spacing, and text styles.
Defines spacing, sizing, border, shadow, animation, breakpoint, and z-index token groups.
Documents JSON, CSS, and SCSS export formats for design token handoff.
Provides guidance for component architecture, responsive scales, accessibility, and developer handoff.
limitations: The bundled Python script currently has a syntax error in the warning color value and needs repair before direct execution.
The script accepts command-line color input without validation, so malformed colors can cause runtime errors.
Dark mode variants are not generated automatically.
The predefined styles are limited to modern, classic, and playful presets.

use_cases.title: Start a design token library
use_cases.description: Create a structured token plan for brand colors, typography, spacing, and responsive behavior.

use_cases.title: Prepare implementation tokens
use_cases.description: Convert design decisions into CSS or SCSS token concepts for application styling.

use_cases.title: Standardize handoff documentation
use_cases.description: Use consistent token categories and component references across design and engineering teams.

prompt_templates.title: Create starter tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a starter UI design token plan for brand color #0066CC using the modern style.

prompt_templates.title: Plan CSS handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare tokens for a web project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CSS-oriented design token handoff for brand color #3B82F6, including colors, type, spacing, and breakpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Audit component consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Review a design system before build
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this token set for component consistency, responsive coverage, accessibility gaps, and developer handoff clarity.

prompt_templates.title: Design multi-team standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced governance for a shared system
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a design system governance plan that defines token naming, component documentation, accessibility checks, and engineering adoption steps.

output_examples.input: Create starter tokens for a modern product using brand color #0066CC.
output: A primary and secondary palette based on the brand color.
Neutral, semantic, and surface color groups for interface states.
Typography, spacing, breakpoint, shadow, border, and motion token groups.
A handoff summary that explains available export targets.

output_examples.input: Prepare a developer handoff for design tokens.
output: A clear token category map for engineering implementation.
Naming guidance for color, type, spacing, and component size values.
Notes for CSS or SCSS usage without prescribing a full build system.

best_practices: Fix the current script syntax error before using the Python helper in automation.
Validate brand color input before generating or exporting token values.
Review generated tokens with designers and developers before adopting them as shared standards.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated tokens as a complete brand system without design review.
Do not mix multiple spacing scales in one product unless a migration plan exists.
Do not publish token exports before checking contrast, responsive behavior, and component usage.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create UI design token structures for colors, typography, spacing, sizing, borders, shadows, animation, and breakpoints.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can the Python helper be run immediately?
faq.answer: Not reliably. The current script contains an unterminated string literal in the warning color token.

faq.question: Does it access the network or local secrets?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network access, secret access, credential reads, or filesystem writes in the reviewed files.

faq.question: What export formats are documented?
faq.answer: The skill documents JSON, CSS, and SCSS export formats for design token handoff.

faq.question: Does it generate dark mode tokens?
faq.answer: No. Dark mode variants are listed as a manual follow-up rather than automatic output.
